capstones.title: The Warm-Up
capstones.premise: A relaxed Saturday at an outdoor coffee market. You spot someone easy to talk to and decide to act on the calm, grounded mindset the path taught you.
capstones.persona: Maya — warm, unhurried, a little shy at first but opens up fast when you're genuine. She rewards calm presence and punishes neediness.
capstones.setting: An outdoor weekend coffee-and-flowers market, mid-morning, low pressure.
capstones.coachIntro: This is your graduation rep for Foundations. No tricks — just show up grounded, present, and warm. Approach before you overthink it.
capstones.coachRecap: Foundations is about the inner game before the words. Watch where nerves pulled you out of the moment, and revisit that lecture.

stages.label: Open
stages.goal: Approach calmly and start without a script
stages.situation: She's choosing between two coffee blends and glances up. You have a natural three-second window.
stages.successCue: You open relaxed and present, reacting to the real moment instead of a canned line.
stages.failCue: You hesitate, over-rehearse, or open with high-pressure energy that makes her brace.

stages.label: Small talk
stages.situation: She responds politely. The conversation could die here or warm up.
stages.successCue: You keep it light and curious, matching her pace so it feels effortless.
stages.failCue: You interview her, run out of thread, or fill silence with nervous chatter.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.situation: She mentions she's here every weekend to reset from a busy job.
stages.successCue: You pick up the thread and share a little of yourself, making it a real exchange.
stages.failCue: You stay transactional and miss the opening to connect.

stages.label: Flirt
stages.goal: Add light warmth without forcing it
stages.situation: There's an easy rapport now and a natural lull.
stages.successCue: You let a little playful warmth in, calibrated to her comfort.
stages.failCue: You either go cold and businesslike or push intensity she didn't signal.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: She's clearly enjoyed this but her coffee's ready.
stages.successCue: You suggest a simple, specific next step with zero neediness.
stages.failCue: You let her walk off with a vague 'maybe see you around' or over-ask.

capstones.title: The Approach
capstones.premise: A quiet bookstore on a weekday evening. Everything rides on your opening and the first sixty seconds of presence.
capstones.persona: Elena — sharp and observant, reads body language instantly. A strong, congruent open earns her attention; a weak one loses it fast.
capstones.setting: An independent bookstore, soft light, near the fiction shelves.
capstones.coachIntro: First Impressions live or die in the first minute. Open with intent, hold your presence, and make the first sixty seconds count.
capstones.coachRecap: If she cooled off, look at your open and your body language in the first minute — that's where impressions lock in.

stages.label: Open
stages.situation: She's reading a back cover near you. You have one clean opening.
stages.successCue: You open with relaxed intent and a natural reason, voice and posture aligned.
stages.failCue: You mumble, hover, or lead with an apology that signals low status.

stages.label: Hold presence
stages.goal: Anchor the first 60 seconds with composure
stages.situation: She looks up, mildly curious, waiting to see who you are.
stages.successCue: You hold steady eye contact and grounded posture, unrushed.
stages.failCue: You fidget, speed up, or break away nervously and deflate the moment.

stages.label: Small talk
stages.goal: Convert the open into a real conversation
stages.situation: The opener landed. Now it needs to become a conversation.
stages.successCue: You bridge smoothly from the opener into genuine curiosity about her.
stages.failCue: You stall after the opener with nothing to follow it.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.goal: Find one point of real connection
stages.situation: She mentions the book is a comfort re-read from a hard year.
stages.successCue: You meet that with warmth and a bit of your own truth.
stages.failCue: You skip past it back to surface chatter.

stages.label: Close
stages.goal: Secure a next step while the spark is fresh
stages.situation: She's about to head to the register.
stages.successCue: You make a confident, specific ask before the window closes.
stages.failCue: You wait too long and lose the moment to hesitation.

capstones.title: The Flowing Chat
capstones.premise: A friend's low-key house party. The whole test is sustaining a conversation that flows — questions, listening, stories, rhythm.
capstones.persona: Priya — talkative and expressive, loves a good back-and-forth. She lights up when you actually listen and build on what she says.
capstones.setting: A friend's kitchen at a relaxed house party, drink in hand.
capstones.coachIntro: This is pure conversation craft. Keep the ball moving — ask, listen, share, and let it breathe. No dead air, no interrogation.
capstones.coachRecap: Where the chat stalled tells you what to revisit — question quality, active listening, or your own storytelling.

stages.label: Open
stages.goal: Start a conversation in a group setting
stages.situation: You end up next to her by the snacks during a lull.
stages.successCue: You open naturally off the shared context with easy energy.
stages.failCue: You force a line that ignores the setting or freeze up.

stages.label: Build flow
stages.goal: Create rhythm with questions and listening
stages.situation: She answers and tosses a question back.
stages.successCue: You listen actively, build on her answers, and keep the rally going.
stages.failCue: You fire rapid questions or wait for your turn instead of listening.

stages.label: Storytelling
stages.situation: She shares something funny and looks to you.
stages.successCue: You match with a tight, vivid story that invites her in.
stages.failCue: You ramble, over-explain, or one-up her instead of connecting.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.goal: Shift from fun to a bit more real
stages.situation: The banter opens a door to something more genuine.
stages.successCue: You take it one notch deeper while keeping the warmth.
stages.failCue: You keep it all surface and the connection plateaus.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: People start leaving and she's gathering her things.
stages.successCue: You suggest continuing the conversation another time, specifically.
stages.failCue: You let a great chat end with no next step.

capstones.title: The Playful Exchange
capstones.premise: An arcade bar on a first date. The path's whole point — banter, teasing, callbacks — gets put to the test in a playful setting.
capstones.persona: Sofia — quick-witted and competitive, gives as good as she gets. She's drawn to playful confidence and bored by safe, flat replies.
capstones.setting: A retro arcade bar, neon and pinball, first date.
capstones.coachIntro: Bring the play. Tease lightly, riff on her, and plant callbacks you can return to. Keep it warm — playful, never cutting.
capstones.coachRecap: If the spark dimmed, check your calibration — teasing that lands stays warm. Revisit callbacks and banter timing.

stages.label: Open
stages.goal: Set a playful tone from the start
stages.situation: She challenges you to pick the first game.
stages.successCue: You set a fun, slightly competitive frame right away.
stages.failCue: You play it overly safe and kill the playful energy.

stages.label: Banter
stages.situation: She gloats after beating you at air hockey.
stages.successCue: You tease back with warmth and roll with hers.
stages.failCue: You get defensive, go flat, or push the teasing too hard.

stages.label: Callback
stages.goal: Plant and reuse an inside joke
stages.situation: Earlier she made a joke about being 'basically a pro.'
stages.successCue: You callback to it at the perfect moment for an easy laugh.
stages.failCue: You let good material die or force a callback that falls flat.

stages.label: Flirt
stages.situation: The teasing has real chemistry under it now.
stages.successCue: You let the banter tip into genuine flirtation.
stages.failCue: You stay in joke-mode and never let it get real.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: The night's winding down on a high note.
stages.successCue: You close playfully and confidently for a second date.
stages.failCue: You undercut the moment with a joke instead of a real ask.

capstones.title: The Read
capstones.premise: A gallery opening. This capstone is about reading her — interest cues, calibration, and timing your escalation to her signals.
capstones.persona: Nadia — subtle and composed; her interest shows in small cues, not big declarations. Misreading her costs you; reading her well opens everything.
capstones.setting: A small art gallery opening, wine in hand, quiet crowd.
capstones.coachIntro: Tonight is about calibration. Read her cues before you move. Escalate when she's giving green lights — pull back when she isn't.
capstones.coachRecap: Mistimed moves point straight to the signals you missed. Revisit the cue-reading and escalation-timing lectures.

stages.label: Open
stages.situation: You comment on a piece she's been studying.
stages.successCue: You open and immediately start reading her response level.
stages.failCue: You barrel in without noticing how she's receiving you.

stages.label: Read signals
stages.situation: She gives mixed signals — polite words, but open body language.
stages.successCue: You weight the genuine cues over the polite ones and read her accurately.
stages.failCue: You misread politeness as disinterest or vice versa.

stages.label: Calibrate
stages.goal: Match your energy to her signals
stages.situation: Her engagement rises as you find a shared interest.
stages.successCue: You dial your energy up to match hers precisely.
stages.failCue: You stay flat when she's warming, or overshoot her level.

stages.label: Escalate
stages.goal: Time the escalation to a green light
stages.situation: She lingers and her cues turn clearly warm.
stages.successCue: You escalate right as the green light appears.
stages.failCue: You escalate too early on a yellow, or miss the window entirely.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: Her interest is unmistakable now.
stages.successCue: You close confidently, riding the clear signal.
stages.failCue: You second-guess the obvious green light and stall.

capstones.title: The Grounded Date
capstones.premise: A rooftop bar. The test is presence itself — body language, vocal delivery, and composure under a little social pressure.
capstones.persona: Camila — grounded and self-assured; she mirrors the energy she's given. Calm, embodied presence draws her in; jitter pushes her back.
capstones.setting: A buzzy rooftop bar at golden hour, slightly loud.
capstones.coachIntro: This one's about how you carry yourself. Slow your voice, ground your body, and stay composed even when it gets a little intense.
capstones.coachRecap: Where composure slipped is your revision target — posture, pacing, or vocal delivery under pressure.

stages.label: Open
stages.situation: You reach the bar at the same time and order beside her.
stages.successCue: You open with steady posture, relaxed pace, and a grounded voice.
stages.failCue: You rush your words or telegraph nerves in your body.

stages.label: Composure
stages.goal: Stay composed when it gets loud and busy
stages.situation: A rowdy group crowds in beside you.
stages.successCue: You hold your calm and keep your attention on her.
stages.failCue: You get flustered or visibly thrown by the chaos.

stages.label: Vocal delivery
stages.goal: Use pacing and tone to hold attention
stages.situation: It's loud and she leans in to hear you.
stages.successCue: You slow down, lower and steady your voice, and draw her in.
stages.failCue: You speed up and raise your pitch, sounding anxious.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.goal: Create an intimate pocket in a loud room
stages.situation: You find a quieter corner of the rooftop.
stages.successCue: You use the calm to deepen the connection with full presence.
stages.failCue: You stay scattered and never settle into the moment.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: The sun's down and the night could end or extend.
stages.successCue: You make a grounded, unhurried close.
stages.failCue: You let nerves rush or fumble the ask.

capstones.title: The Real Conversation
capstones.premise: A quiet wine bar, second date. This is about depth — vulnerability, catching her bids, and building real emotional connection.
capstones.persona: Hannah — thoughtful and emotionally present; she opens up when you do and notices when you dodge depth. Bids for connection are everywhere if you catch them.
capstones.setting: A dim, quiet wine bar, second date, no time pressure.
capstones.coachIntro: Tonight you go deep. Share something real, catch her bids for connection, and let the conversation matter. No deflecting.
capstones.coachRecap: Missed bids or dodged vulnerability are your revision targets — that's where depth broke down.

stages.label: Open
stages.situation: You settle in and she asks how your week really was.
stages.successCue: You answer honestly instead of with a reflexive 'fine.'
stages.failCue: You keep it surface and signal the night will stay shallow.

stages.label: Catch a bid
stages.goal: Notice and turn toward her bid
stages.situation: She mentions, almost in passing, that she's been homesick lately.
stages.successCue: You catch the bid and turn toward it with genuine attention.
stages.failCue: You miss it or turn away to a lighter topic.

stages.label: Be vulnerable
stages.goal: Share something real of your own
stages.situation: The moment invites you to reciprocate her openness.
stages.successCue: You share something genuine and a little vulnerable.
stages.failCue: You stay guarded and break the reciprocity.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.situation: You're both being real now and it's landing.
stages.successCue: You stay in it and let the closeness build naturally.
stages.failCue: You get uncomfortable and pull back to safety.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: The night feels like it meant something.
stages.successCue: You name that it was a real evening and lock in the next.
stages.failCue: You undercut the depth with a flippant goodbye.

capstones.title: The Cool Head
capstones.premise: A first date that throws curveballs. The whole test is nerve management, non-neediness, and recovering smoothly when things go sideways.
capstones.persona: Jordan — friendly but unpredictable; she runs late, shifts plans, and watches how you handle it. Composure and non-neediness win; reactivity loses.
capstones.setting: A casual cocktail spot — except things don't go to plan.
capstones.coachIntro: Expect curveballs tonight. Manage your nerves, stay non-needy, and recover gracefully when something goes wrong. Unflappable is the goal.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever you got reactive or needy is the lecture to revisit — that's where your cool head slipped.

stages.label: Open (she's late)
stages.goal: Handle a rocky start without leaking nerves
stages.situation: She arrives 20 minutes late and a little frazzled.
stages.successCue: You stay warm and unbothered, putting her at ease.
stages.failCue: You sulk, over-reassure, or make her feel guilty.

stages.label: Manage nerves
stages.situation: The rocky start spikes your nerves mid-conversation.
stages.successCue: You self-regulate and stay present instead of spiraling.
stages.failCue: You get in your head and go quiet or overcompensate.

stages.label: Non-neediness
stages.goal: Hold your frame without seeking approval
stages.situation: She gives a noncommittal answer about whether she's enjoying herself.
stages.successCue: You stay secure and don't chase her validation.
stages.failCue: You start performing and fishing for reassurance.

stages.label: Recover
stages.goal: Bounce back from an awkward moment
stages.situation: A joke lands flat and the energy dips.
stages.successCue: You recover smoothly and reset the mood with ease.
stages.failCue: You dwell on it and let it sink the date.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: Despite the rough patches, there's real warmth now.
stages.successCue: You close with calm confidence, unfazed by the bumps.
stages.failCue: You let the earlier chaos make you tentative.

capstones.title: Your Secure Self
capstones.premise: A few weeks into seeing someone, a moment of uncertainty hits. This capstone tests attachment-aware, secure communication under emotional pressure.
capstones.persona: Rebecca — warm but independent; she occasionally needs space, which can trigger an anxious or avoidant reaction in you. Secure responses deepen trust.
capstones.setting: A walk in the park after she's been a little distant over text this week.
capstones.coachIntro: This rep tests your secure self. When her behavior pings your attachment system, regulate first, then communicate from security — not protest, not withdrawal.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever anxiety or avoidance hijacked you is your revision target. Revisit the attachment-aware communication lectures.

stages.label: Open
stages.situation: She greets you a little reserved after a quiet week.
stages.successCue: You reconnect with warmth and zero accusation.
stages.failCue: You open cold, testy, or visibly wounded.

stages.label: Notice the trigger
stages.situation: She mentions she needed space this week, and you feel the pang.
stages.successCue: You notice the reaction in yourself before it drives you.
stages.failCue: You react on autopilot — protest or shut down.

stages.label: Self-regulate
stages.situation: The urge to either cling or punish is right there.
stages.successCue: You self-soothe and choose a secure response.
stages.failCue: You let the urge run the conversation.

stages.label: Communicate securely
stages.situation: It's time to say how the distance felt.
stages.successCue: You name your need calmly and respect her autonomy.
stages.failCue: You accuse, guilt-trip, or bury the need entirely.

stages.label: Close
stages.situation: The talk could bring you closer or create distance.
stages.successCue: You close with mutual understanding and warmth.
stages.failCue: You leave it tense or falsely smoothed over.

capstones.title: The Full Thread
capstones.premise: A text-only capstone. You match with someone and must run the entire thread — open, build rapport, flirt, and ask her out — over messages.
capstones.persona: Ava — busy and a little hard to pin down over text; she replies to messages that have energy and personality, and goes quiet on boring ones.
capstones.setting: A messaging thread after matching on a dating app.
capstones.coachIntro: Text-only tonight. Every message has to earn the next reply. Open with personality, build rapport, flirt, then ask her out cleanly.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever the thread went cold points to the lecture to revisit — opener, rapport, flirting, or the ask.

stages.label: Opener
stages.goal: Send an opener that earns a reply
stages.situation: You just matched. Her profile mentions she's training for a half-marathon.
stages.successCue: You open with specific, playful personality tied to her profile.
stages.failCue: You send 'hey' or a generic line that's easy to ignore.

stages.label: Build rapport
stages.situation: She replies with interest and a question.
stages.successCue: You build a genuine rhythm and keep it light and engaging.
stages.failCue: You go dry, interview her, or take hours of low-effort replies.

stages.label: Flirt
stages.situation: The rapport is good and there's room to flirt.
stages.successCue: You introduce light, calibrated flirtation.
stages.failCue: You stay flat and friend-zone the thread, or get crude.

stages.label: Ask out
stages.situation: The energy is high — time to move it offline.
stages.successCue: You ask her out with a specific, low-friction plan.
stages.failCue: You stall, over-text, or ask vaguely and lose momentum.

stages.label: Lock logistics
stages.situation: She says yes and asks about details.
stages.successCue: You lock day, time, and place without flakiness.
stages.failCue: You leave it loose and let the plan drift toward a flake.

capstones.title: The Profile Audit
capstones.premise: A presentation-forward capstone. Instead of a date, you build and defend an end-to-end dating-app presence — photos, bio, prompts, and opener strategy — scored on congruence and positioning.
capstones.persona: Coach review panel — evaluates your profile the way a sharp, busy woman swiping would: in seconds, on signal and congruence.
capstones.setting: A profile-builder review session, swipe's-eye view.
capstones.coachIntro: No date here — this is your profile under the microscope. Make every photo, line, and prompt pull its weight and stay congruent with who you actually are.
capstones.coachRecap: Whichever element scored weak — photos, bio, prompts, or strategy — is the lecture to revisit.

stages.label: Photo lineup
stages.goal: Sequence photos that tell a clear story
stages.situation: You have six photos to order into a lineup.
stages.successCue: You lead with a strong, clear shot and build a varied, honest story.
stages.failCue: You lead weak, repeat the same look, or include red-flag shots.

stages.label: Bio
stages.goal: Write a bio with signal and personality
stages.situation: You have a few lines to land who you are.
stages.successCue: You write something specific, confident, and inviting.
stages.failCue: You go generic, negative, or try-hard.

stages.label: Prompts
stages.goal: Answer prompts that invite a reply
stages.situation: Three prompts need answers.
stages.successCue: You give hooks that are easy and fun to respond to.
stages.failCue: You give closed, bland, or bragging answers.

stages.label: Positioning
stages.situation: The pieces need to add up to one coherent person.
stages.successCue: Every element reinforces the same authentic positioning.
stages.failCue: The pieces contradict each other and read as inauthentic.

stages.label: Opener strategy
stages.situation: You define your first-message approach.
stages.successCue: You commit to specific, personalized opener tactics.
stages.failCue: You default to copy-paste openers that get ignored.

capstones.title: The Whole Date
capstones.premise: End-to-end first-date logistics: plan it, manage pre-date nerves, run the date, close, and follow up the next day. The full arc in one rep.
capstones.persona: Grace — easygoing but observant; she notices when a date is well-planned and when follow-through is thoughtful. Logistics signal effort to her.
capstones.setting: From planning through the day after a first date at a wine-and-tapas spot.
capstones.coachIntro: This is the whole arc — plan, nerves, date, close, follow-up. Logistics are part of the impression, so handle every phase with intention.
capstones.coachRecap: Whichever phase dragged — planning, nerves, the date, or follow-up — is your revision target.

stages.label: Plan the date
stages.situation: You're picking where and when to meet.
stages.successCue: You propose a well-judged plan that's easy to say yes to.
stages.failCue: You pick something high-pressure, vague, or low-effort.

stages.label: Pre-date nerves
stages.situation: An hour before, the nerves are building.
stages.successCue: You manage your state and arrive grounded.
stages.failCue: You show up frazzled or numbed-out.

stages.label: The date
stages.situation: You're at the table and it's underway.
stages.successCue: You apply your conversation and presence skills throughout.
stages.failCue: You let logistics anxiety crowd out real connection.

stages.label: Close
stages.goal: End the date with a clear next step
stages.situation: The check comes and the night's ending.
stages.successCue: You close warmly with a specific second-date seed.
stages.failCue: You end on a vague note with no momentum.

stages.label: Follow-up
stages.situation: It's the next morning.
stages.successCue: You follow up with warmth and a concrete next step.
stages.failCue: You go silent, over-text, or send something flat.

capstones.title: The Next Chapter
capstones.premise: Turning dating into a relationship: second-date momentum, deepening disclosure, navigating pace, and the exclusivity talk — with secure repair if it gets bumpy.
capstones.persona: Lauren — genuinely interested but careful; she wants honesty about where things are going and moves at her own considered pace.
capstones.setting: A series of moments across a deepening connection, culminating in the exclusivity talk.
capstones.coachIntro: This rep takes you from second date to defining the relationship. Deepen honestly, respect her pace, and have the exclusivity talk directly and warmly.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever it wobbled — deepening, pace, or the talk — points to the lecture to revisit.

stages.label: Second date
stages.goal: Build on the first with momentum
stages.situation: You're on date two, referencing a moment from date one.
stages.successCue: You build continuity and warmth from the first date.
stages.failCue: You treat it like a cold restart with no callback.

stages.label: Deepen
stages.situation: There's room to go a layer deeper than last time.
stages.successCue: You share more and invite her to as well.
stages.failCue: You stay in the same surface register as date one.

stages.label: Navigate pace
stages.situation: You want more than she seems ready for right now.
stages.successCue: You state your need while respecting her autonomy.
stages.failCue: You pressure her or silently swallow the need.

stages.label: Exclusivity talk
stages.situation: The moment to name what you want has arrived.
stages.successCue: You say it clearly and warmly, inviting an honest answer.
stages.failCue: You hint anxiously or dodge the talk entirely.

stages.label: Secure repair
stages.goal: Repair if the talk gets tense
stages.situation: A small friction surfaces during the talk.
stages.successCue: You soften, own your part, and reconnect.
stages.failCue: You get defensive and let it harden into distance.

capstones.title: The Put-Together Debut
capstones.premise: A presentation-forward capstone on self-presentation: grooming, dressing for context, fit, and a signature look — scored on enclothed confidence and congruence, not a date.
capstones.persona: Coach styling review — assesses your presentation the way a first impression reads it: head-to-toe, in seconds, for self-respect and congruence.
capstones.setting: A get-ready review before a real date, mirror's-eye view.
capstones.coachIntro: No conversation here — this is your presentation graded. Grooming, fit, and a look that's authentically yours. Make the outside match the inside.
capstones.coachRecap: Whichever element fell short — grooming, context-dressing, fit, or signature look — is the lecture to revisit.

stages.label: Grooming check
stages.situation: You're prepping the fundamentals before getting dressed.
stages.successCue: Your grooming is clean, deliberate, and self-respecting.
stages.failCue: You skip basics or over-do it into try-hard.

stages.label: Dress for context
stages.goal: Match the outfit to the occasion
stages.situation: The date is a casual-but-nice dinner.
stages.successCue: You read the context and dress appropriately for it.
stages.failCue: You over- or under-dress for the setting.

stages.label: Fit
stages.situation: You're choosing between a few options.
stages.successCue: You pick pieces that fit your body well.
stages.failCue: You go baggy, tight, or ill-fitting.

stages.label: Signature look
stages.situation: You add the details that make it yours.
stages.successCue: You add a signature touch that's genuinely you.
stages.failCue: You copy a trend that feels like a costume.

stages.label: Congruence
stages.goal: Make the look match the man
stages.situation: Final mirror check before heading out.
stages.successCue: Your look and your demeanor line up and read as confident.
stages.failCue: The polish contradicts how you carry yourself.

capstones.title: The Attuned Date
capstones.premise: An emotional-intelligence capstone: read her state, regulate yourself when something stings, respond with empathy, catch a bid, and repair a misstep — scored on attunement, not lines.
capstones.persona: Isabel — emotionally expressive and perceptive; she responds to being truly seen and notices instantly when you're reactive or tuned out.
capstones.setting: A dinner date that moves through real emotional terrain.
capstones.coachIntro: This rep grades attunement. Read her, regulate yourself when it stings, respond with empathy, catch her bids, and repair cleanly if you slip.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever attunement broke — reading, regulating, empathizing, or repairing — is your revision target.

stages.label: Read her state
stages.situation: She arrives upbeat but with something clearly on her mind.
stages.successCue: You notice the undercurrent and gently make room for it.
stages.failCue: You miss it entirely and barrel into your own agenda.

stages.label: Self-regulate
stages.situation: She says something that lands a little sharp.
stages.successCue: You pause and self-regulate before responding.
stages.failCue: You react defensively in the heat of it.

stages.label: Empathic response
stages.situation: She opens up about a stressful situation at work.
stages.successCue: You take her perspective and respond with genuine empathy.
stages.failCue: You jump to fixing or make it about you.

stages.label: Catch a bid
stages.goal: Turn toward her bid for connection
stages.situation: She makes a small, hopeful bid for closeness.
stages.successCue: You catch it and turn toward her warmly.
stages.failCue: You turn away or miss it.

stages.label: Repair
stages.situation: You realize one response landed wrong earlier.
stages.successCue: You name it, own it, and reconnect.
stages.failCue: You let it sit and the distance lingers.

capstones.title: Your Authentic Date
capstones.premise: The inclusive-paths capstone: run a date inside your own dating context, adapting the core skills — warmth, authenticity, attunement — confidently to who you are and who you're dating.
capstones.persona: Adaptive — the persona reflects the user's own stated dating context; warm and real, responding to authenticity and respect for where each person is.
capstones.setting: A date set in the user's own chosen context and terms.
capstones.coachIntro: This is your date, your context. Apply everything you've learned authentically — open as yourself, communicate openly, respect where each person is, and build a connection that fits your life.
capstones.coachRecap: Wherever it felt forced or scripted, revisit how to adapt the core skills to your own authentic context.

stages.label: Open authentically
stages.situation: The date begins in your own setting and terms.
stages.successCue: You open warm and authentic, not from a borrowed script.
stages.failCue: You force a template that doesn't fit you.

stages.label: Identity & intentions
stages.goal: Communicate openly and ask with curiosity
stages.situation: The conversation reaches identity and what you're each looking for.
stages.successCue: You share where you're at and ask with genuine curiosity.
stages.failCue: You assume her identity or intentions instead of asking.

stages.label: Respect visibility
stages.situation: Comfort with being public differs between you.
stages.successCue: You respect her stage with zero pressure.
stages.failCue: You push her to be more or less open than she's ready for.

stages.label: Build connection
stages.situation: The date is flowing and real.
stages.successCue: You bring warmth, attunement, and presence in your own voice.
stages.failCue: You disappear behind a persona that isn't you.

stages.label: Close on your terms
stages.goal: Define a next step that fits your life
stages.situation: The date is ending on a genuine note.
stages.successCue: You close toward a connection shaped by your own values.
stages.failCue: You default to a script that doesn't match what you actually want.
